sentences.text: Ladies and gentlemen, welcome to Vienna, the capital of Austria and a city of rich history, culture and elegance.
My name is Maximilian Fischer and I'll be your tour guide.
Let me give you a brief overview of what you can expect during our tour.
Our first stop in Vienna will be St. Stephen's Cathedral, or as the locals call it, Stéphane's Dom.
The cathedral is almost 200 meters long, 62 meters wide and 136 meters high.
There are many stands.
There are statues and figures around the building.
There is a part of the cathedral that dates back to the 12th century.
However, the most of St. Stephen's Cathedral building isn't quite as old.
The second oldest part of St. Stephen's Cathedral is the main entrance door, which was created in 1263.
Over the centuries, several towers and other additions to the building were built.
From there, we will head to the Hofburg Palace, the former imperial residence of the Habsburg dynasty
and the former imperial residence of the Habsburg dynasty.
The Hofburg Palace is located in the center of Vienna.
It was built in the 13th century and expanded several times afterwards.
It also served as the imperial winter residence, as another palace called Schönbrunn Palace was the summer residence.
Since 1946, the Hofburg Palace is the official residence and workplace of the president of Austria.
The Hofburg Palace houses some of the most important museums in the city,
including the Sisi Museum dedicated to Emerson.
The Hofburg Palace is the home of the Empress Elizabeth.
Additionally, visitors can explore the imperial apartments to see how the royalty lived.
The entrance fee to the Hofburg Palace is €11 for visitors under 18 and €17.50 for visitors above 19.
The entrance fee with audio guide to the Sisi Museum is €20.50 for adults and €13 for children aged between 6 and 18.
No visit to Vienna would be complete without a walk along the famous Grand Hall.
The Hofburg Palace is located along the faithful and historic Rindstrasse Boulevard.
Along the way, you'll see iconic sites such as the Vienna State Opera, the Parliament Building, and many more.
Construction of the Vienna State Opera was paid by Emperor Franz Josef in 1861.
We will also pass by the parliament building of Vienna, which is worth visiting of course.
Before we complete our tour, we'll visit the Belvedere Palace.
The Belvedere Palace is a home to two German masters and their daughter, Anna.
The Belvedere Palace is a home to two German masters and their daughter, Anna.
an amazing collection of art. The part of the permanent exhibition, Gustav Klimt's painting
called The Kiss, is more than just a work of art. This ageless painting is a representation of love
and the human spirit. The Austrian state purchased The Kiss. So, with a total of 24 works,
the Belvedere owns the biggest and most important collection of Klimt paintings in the world.
We can't forget about Viennese coffee culture. Be sure to try a slice of the famous chocolate
cake called Sachertorte at Café Sacher. Also visit the excellent and unforgettable Café Central,
or Café Korb, which is designed by several artists. Don't miss the opportunity to enjoy
the best cup of coffee at one of the city's traditional coffee houses called Lantmann.
Café Lantmann was opened in 1873, and till today it offers an outstanding
coffee culture. The coffee culture is a part of the Viennese coffee culture.
The highest quality standards and hospitality right in the heart of Vienna.
One cannot forget about famous composers like Mozart and Beethoven in Vienna.
You can find statues of famous composers in the parks of Vienna.
I recommend to attend one of classical music concerts, which are held in many concert halls.
You can buy concert tickets online or directly in the Vienna State Opera.
Please feel free to ask any questions along the way, and let's begin
this journey.
Vienna awaits!
